tempssm 0.2.4
  • Reference
    • tempssm: State-space modeling for temperature time series in R
  • Changelog

Run time series cross-validation for a single fold

Source: R/ts_cross_validation.R
ts_cv_run_fold.Rd

Fits a state space model using tempssm() on a single training/test split generated by ts_train_test_split(), and produces forecasts for the corresponding test period.

This function is primarily used internally by ts_cv_run() as part of the cross-validation workflow. However, it is also exported for advanced users who wish to inspect or debug model behavior on individual folds.

For most use cases, users should call ts_cv_run() instead of using this function directly.

For models without exogenous variables, forecasts are generated using predict(..., n.ahead = h). When exogenous variables are present, a new SSModel object is constructed for the test period using the estimated parameters.

Note: The current implementation for models with exogenous variables relies on explicit reconstruction of SSModel(), and may be sensitive to model specification. This part is subject to future improvement.

Usage

ts_cv_run_fold(fold, ar_order = 1, use_season = TRUE)

Arguments

fold

A single fold object returned by ts_train_test_split().

ar_order

Integer scalar specifying the order of the autoregressive (AR) component in the error structure (e.g., 2 for AR(2), 3 for AR(3)). Defaults to 1. Orders from 1 to 4 are intended as the usual working range; larger values are allowed but trigger a warning because they may lead to unstable estimation.

use_season

Logical scalar; should the seasonal component be considered? Defaults to TRUE.

Value

A named list with the following components:

fold

Integer fold index.

converged

Logical scalar indicating whether fitting succeeded.

y_train

Training time series of class ts.

y_test

Test time series of class ts.

y_pred

Predicted values for the test period, as returned by stats::predict().

model

A fitted state-space model, or NULL if fitting failed.

Details

When no exogenous variables are supplied, forecasts are obtained directly via predict() with n.ahead. In this case, future exogenous effects are implicitly treated as zero.

When exogenous variables are present, forecasts depend on the provided test-period covariates. Users should ensure that exo_test_ts represents realistic or observed future values.

See also

ts_cv_run for running cross-validation over all folds.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
data(yamaguchi_sst)

# create folds (no exogenous variables)
folds <- ts_train_test_split(
  temp_data = yamaguchi_sst,
  initial   = 60,
  horizon   = 12,
  step      = 12
)

res <- ts_cv_run_fold(folds[[1]])

# inspect predictions
res$y_pred

# compare observed vs predicted
cbind(
  observed = as.numeric(res$y_test),
  predicted = as.numeric(res$y_pred)
)
} # }
